text: The general user shall be able to monitor and control the home environment.
A Master user shall be able to change the system configuration.
A Master user shall be able to add a user account.
A Master user shall be able to change default parameter settings.
The DH Technician shall be able to set up and maintain the system configuration.
The DH Technician shall be able to start and stop the DH System operation.
The DH System shall establish an individual home web server hosted on a home computer.
The DH home web server shall provide interaction with and control of the DH elements.
The DH home web server shall provide storage of DH plans and data.
The DH home web server shall maintain DH User Accounts.
The DH home web server shall provide backup service for user account information and plans.
The DH Gateway device shall provide communication with all DigitalHome devices.
The DH Gateway device shall connect with a broadband Internet connection.
The DH Gateway device shall include an RF Module for wireless communication with sensors and controllers.
The DH Gateway device shall operate up to a 1000-foot range for indoor transmission.
The system shall include digital programmable thermostats to monitor and regulate temperature.
The thermostat shall provide a reading of the current temperature.
The thermostat shall provide a set point temperature to control heating or cooling devices.
The thermostat sensor shall have a sensitivity range between 14°F and 104°F.
The system shall include digital programmable humidistats to monitor and regulate humidity.
The humidistat shall provide a reading of the current humidity.
The humidistat shall provide a set point humidity to control humidifiers and dehumidifiers.
The system shall include magnetic alarm contact switches to monitor entry through doors or windows.
The system shall include security sound and light alarms activated by a security breach.
The system shall include digital programmable power switches to monitor appliance states.
The system shall change the state of an appliance using a power switch.
The DH user shall be able to read the temperature at a thermostat position.
The DH user shall be able to set thermostat temperatures between 60°F and 80°F inclusive in one degree increments.
The system shall support up to eight thermostats per home.
The system shall schedule up to twenty-four one-hour thermostat settings per day for every day of the week.
The system shall support Fahrenheit and Celsius temperature values.
The DH user shall be able to read the humidity at a humidistat position.
The DH user shall be able to set the humidity level from 30% to 60% inclusive in 1% increments.
The system shall support up to eight humidistats per home.
The system shall schedule up to twenty-four one-hour humidistat settings per day for every day of the week.
The DH system shall manage up to fifty door and window contact sensors.
The Appliance Manager shall manage up to one hundred power switches.
The DH Planner shall provide the capability to set preset parameters for specific time periods.
The DH Planner shall provide a monthly plan for up to four daily time periods.
The system shall provide a report on home management for any month in the past two years.
The month report shall contain daily average, maximum, and minimum values for temperature and humidity.
The month report shall provide the day and time of any security breaches.
The month report shall indicate periods when the DH System was not in operation.
The system shall update environmental condition displays at least every two seconds.
Environmental sensors shall have a minimum data acquisition rate of 10 Hz.
The DH System shall have no more than 1 failure per 10,000 hours of operation.
The DH System shall backup all system data on a daily basis.
The system recovery mechanism shall restore system data from the most recent backup after a failure.
The system shall respond with a clear descriptive message when an exceptional condition occurs.
The DH web system shall provide authentication and information encryption using Transport Layer Security.
The system shall require an account name and a password for login.
The DH system shall provide online documentation including an FAQ section.
